variations.standard: General sanctions compliance warranty with key regimes listed
variations.enhanced: Includes ongoing screening, certification, and subcontractor flow-down
variations.multi-regime: Comprehensive coverage of US, UK, EU, and UN sanctions

clause_types.name: Export Control Compliance
clause_types.description: Requires compliance with applicable export control laws and regulations governing the transfer of controlled goods, software, technology, and technical data across borders. Covers the US Export Administration Regulations (EAR), International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), and EU Dual-Use Regulation.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Reference specific export control regimes (US EAR, US ITAR, EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821, Wassenaar Arrangement). Identify any export-controlled items or technology included in the deliverables. Specify ECCN/export classification numbers where known. Require the receiving party to comply with all applicable export control requirements. Prohibit re-export or re-transfer without required authorisations. Address deemed export rules (technology access by foreign nationals). Require end-user certificates where applicable. Address the interaction with sanctions compliance. Specify record-keeping and reporting obligations.

variations.technology-specific: Includes ECCN classification and specific control requirements
variations.us-centric: Focused on EAR and ITAR compliance

clause_types.name: Work Product Ownership / IP Assignment
clause_types.description: Specifies that all intellectual property created by the provider specifically for the customer under the agreement (foreground IP / work product) is owned by the customer. Provider assigns all rights, title, and interest in the work product to the customer upon creation or payment.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Define 'work product' or 'foreground IP' precisely (IP created specifically for the customer under the agreement). Distinguish from provider's background IP and general know-how. Specify the trigger for assignment (upon creation, upon delivery, or upon payment). Include present assignment language ('hereby assigns' not 'agrees to assign'). Address moral rights (waiver where permitted, consent where waiver is not possible). Require execution of confirmatory assignment documents. Address IP created by subcontractors (require equivalent assignment from subs). Consider the impact on the provider's ability to serve other clients. Coordinate with the license-back provision to ensure the provider retains necessary usage rights.

variations.full-assignment: All work product assigned to customer absolutely
variations.joint-development: Jointly created IP with agreed allocation

clause_types.name: Background IP Reservation
clause_types.description: Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing intellectual property (background IP), tools, methodologies, frameworks, and general know-how that exist prior to or are developed independently of the engagement. Customer receives a license to use background IP only to the extent incorporated into the deliverables.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Define 'background IP' clearly (pre-existing IP, tools, methodologies, libraries, frameworks). Require the provider to identify material background IP incorporated into deliverables (ideally in a schedule). Grant the customer a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive license to use background IP as incorporated into the deliverables, but only for the customer's internal business purposes. Address modifications to background IP made during the engagement (typically remain provider's property with license to customer). Ensure the license survives termination. Address the right to sublicense (affiliates, successor organisations). Consider escrow arrangements for critical background IP.

variations.standard: Provider retains background IP with license to customer for deliverables use
variations.scheduled: Background IP identified in a schedule attached to the agreement
variations.broad-license: Wide license to customer including modification and sublicensing rights

clause_types.name: License-Back to Provider
clause_types.description: Where work product IP is assigned to the customer, the customer grants the provider a non-exclusive, perpetual license to use the work product (or elements thereof) for the provider's internal purposes, including serving other clients, improving its methodologies, and developing derivative works. Enables the provider to benefit from its work without retaining ownership.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Define the scope of the license-back (internal use only, or including use for other clients). Specify restrictions (no licensing to competitors, no disclosure of customer-specific data). Address whether the license-back includes the right to create derivative works. Ensure the license-back does not undermine the customer's ownership rights. Consider whether the license-back should be royalty-free or subject to fees. Address confidentiality obligations regarding the customer's information embedded in the work product. Specify whether the license-back survives termination. Consider excluding customer-specific configurations and data from the license-back.

variations.internal-only: Provider may use only for internal purposes (R&D, training)
variations.broad: Provider may use for other clients and commercial purposes (excluding customer data)
variations.derivative-works: Includes the right to create derivative works

clause_types.name: Improvements and Enhancements
clause_types.description: Addresses ownership and usage rights for improvements, enhancements, and derivative works created during the engagement. Improvements to the provider's background IP typically remain the provider's property, while improvements to the customer's foreground IP are assigned to the customer.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Define 'improvements' and 'enhancements' clearly. Distinguish between improvements to background IP (typically remain with provider) and improvements to foreground IP (typically assigned to customer). Address improvements that cannot be easily attributed to background or foreground IP. Specify the license terms for improvements retained by the provider but incorporated into deliverables. Consider whether the customer has first right of refusal for improvements developed by the provider that are relevant to the customer's use case. Address the treatment of improvements suggested by the customer but implemented by the provider. Coordinate with the work product ownership and license-back provisions.

variations.customer-owns-all: All improvements assigned to customer regardless of origin
variations.follows-base: Improvements follow ownership of the base IP

clause_types.name: IP Non-Assertion Covenant
clause_types.description: A covenant by which one party agrees not to assert its intellectual property rights against the other party in connection with the other party's authorized use of the deliverables or services under the agreement. Provides additional comfort beyond the non-infringement warranty.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Define the scope of the non-assertion covenant precisely (limited to IP embodied in the deliverables and services, not all IP owned by the granting party). Specify the permitted use that is covered by the covenant. Address whether the covenant extends to affiliates and successors. Consider whether the covenant survives termination and for how long. Address the interaction with patent licenses and cross-licenses. Specify exceptions (defensive assertion if the other party initiates patent litigation). Consider the anti-trust implications of broad non-assertion covenants. Coordinate with the license grant and non-infringement warranty.

variations.narrow: Limited to IP embodied in specific deliverables
variations.broad: Covers all IP that could be asserted against the other party's authorised use
variations.defensive-only: Non-assertion except in response to IP claims by the other party

clause_types.name: Data Processing Instructions
clause_types.description: Establishes that the processor shall process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, unless required by EU or Member State law. Defines the scope, nature, and purpose of processing, the types of personal data, and the categories of data subjects.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Document the subject matter, duration, nature and purpose of processing, types of personal data, and categories of data subjects (typically in a schedule). Specify that processing is limited to documented instructions. Address what constitutes 'documented instructions' (the agreement itself, written orders, permitted purposes). Include the processor's obligation to inform the controller if an instruction infringes GDPR. Address the process for issuing additional instructions. Specify that the processor must not process personal data for its own purposes. Include GDPR Art 28(3)(a) compliant language.

variations.scheduled: Processing details in a separate schedule/annex
variations.embedded: Processing details within the DPA body
variations.with-sub-instructions: Includes process for issuing additional instructions during the term

clause_types.name: Security Measures (Art 32 GDPR)
clause_types.description: Requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk of processing, taking into account the state of the art, costs, and the nature, scope, context, and purposes of processing.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Reference GDPR Art 32 requirements. Specify the minimum security measures (pseudonymisation, encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, logging, backup and recovery, regular testing). Include specific measures in a technical and organisational measures annex. Require the processor to maintain and update measures to address evolving threats. Specify the processor's obligation to notify the controller of material changes to security measures. Address employee security obligations (background checks, training, confidentiality agreements). Include the right to audit security measures. Address security certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2) as evidence of compliance.

variations.annex-based: Detailed measures in a separate technical annex
variations.risk-based: Measures proportionate to the risk of processing

clause_types.name: Data Breach Notification
clause_types.description: Requires the processor to notify the controller without undue delay upon becoming aware of a personal data breach. The notification must include details of the breach, the likely consequences, and the measures taken or proposed to address the breach and mitigate its effects.
clause_types.drafting_guidance: Specify the notification timeline (without undue delay, and in any event within a specified number of hours, e.g., 24-72 hours). Define what constitutes a 'personal data breach' (aligned with GDPR Art 4(12)). Specify the minimum information to include in the notification: nature of the breach, categories and approximate number of data subjects affected, likely consequences, measures taken or proposed. Address the processor's obligation to cooperate with the controller's breach response. Specify ongoing information obligations as more details become available. Address the preservation of evidence. Coordinate with the controller's obligations under GDPR Art 33 (supervisory authority notification within 72 hours).

variations.without-undue-delay: GDPR standard language without specific hour requirement
